prompt:
You are extracting atomic facts from a topic document.

The topic document below contains all source text for one specific topic, assembled with provenance headings. Your output must pass all Five Tests.

**Test 1 — Stand-Alone**: A reader with no other facts must fully understand the policy. No implied "who does this apply to?", "when does it apply?", "except what?". Include the subject explicitly in every fact.

**Test 2 — One Topic**: A single noun phrase names the subject. If "and" is needed to name the subject, split into two facts.

**Test 3 — Reconstruction**: Concatenating all your facts must be informationally equivalent to the topic document: same obligations, permissions, exceptions, numerical values. No information omitted, none added.

**Test 4 — Canonical Form**: Active voice. No document-navigation language ("as mentioned in", "refer to", "see above", "this section"). No redundant preamble ("it is important to note that", "this policy states", "please be aware").

**Test 5 — Template-Slot Ready**: No audience-specific framing baked in ("as a parent, you should…", "staff are reminded that…"). No channel-specific framing ("as you read this handbook…").

Instructions:
- This is the only pass that writes content — rewrite source text into canonical form
- Eliminate redundancy across provenance spans (same obligation stated in multiple spans → one fact)
- Split only at true topic boundaries (when the subject noun phrase changes)
- `title`: short noun phrase naming the subject of this fact
- `kind`: assign ONLY for clearly non-policy content — 'rationale', 'principle', or 'context'. Leave null for all policy facts (rules, procedures, fees, contact details, definitions)
- `topic_tags`: kebab-case tags, consistent with the topic_slug used in grouping

Audience scope: staff
Channel scope: staff-handbook, policy-documents

Topic document:
### Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency > Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency

Before and during the outing, remind students to notify staff when they need to go to a different place (e.g., the toilet), as adults need to know where children are at all times. Students must always go in a minimum of two.

### Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency > Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency

Count children every time they change activity or move from one place to another (e.g., before lunch, after lunch, when moving from a closed space to an open area) to ensure all children are present.
raw_response: {"facts":[{"title":"Student notification requirement","content":"Students participating in an outing must notify staff when they need to go to a different place, such as the toilet, ensuring adults are always aware of their whereabouts.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["procedure-in-case-of-an-accident-or-emergency"]},{"title":"Buddy system for students","content":"Students must adhere to a buddy system by always moving in groups of at least two for safety during outings.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["procedure-in-case-of-an-accident-or-emergency"]},{"title":"Student headcount protocol","content":"Staff must count all children every time they change activities or move from one place to another, such as before lunch, after lunch, or when moving from a closed space to an open area, to ensure all children are present.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["procedure-in-case-of-an-accident-or-emergency"]}]}

facts.title: Student notification requirement
facts.content: Students participating in an outing must notify staff when they need to go to a different place, such as the toilet, ensuring adults are always aware of their whereabouts.

facts.title: Buddy system for students
facts.content: Students must adhere to a buddy system by always moving in groups of at least two for safety during outings.

facts.title: Student headcount protocol
facts.content: Staff must count all children every time they change activities or move from one place to another, such as before lunch, after lunch, or when moving from a closed space to an open area, to ensure all children are present.
